channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Oil Prices Rise on Possible Deal to Suspend Attacks in Gulf
item.description: Oil prices edged higher after an American official said the U.S. and Iran had agreed to halt attacks in a key shipping lane, raising hopes that hostilities would not escalate.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How The Times/Siena Poll Writes Sensitive Questions
item.description: Asking voters about highly charged issues is a delicate task. Precise language helps us avoid introducing bias so that we can best understand what is driving voters’ decisions.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Igielnik

media:content.media:description: Graham Platner has addressed several scandals during his United States Senate campaign. Asking poll questions about issues like that can be difficult.

item.title: Poll Shows Maine’s Quirky Political Contours
item.description: A Times/Siena survey reveals a tight race in a key contest for Senate control.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner’s backers are giving him a slim lead over Susan Collins.

item.title: The Big Changes Coming to the Times/Siena Poll
item.description: The goal is to make the survey more deeply representative of the population.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn

media:content.media:description: A new polling technique looks at the question: How many people in the population are like this one survey respondent?

item.title: Platner Has Slight Edge Over Collins in Tight Maine Senate Race, Poll Finds
item.description: The Maine race, which both parties see as key to winning control of the Senate, is extremely close as the sprint to fall begins, a Times/Portland Press Herald/Siena poll found.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Taylor Robinson

item.title: Word of the Day: lassitude
item.description: This word has appeared in two articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Cross-Tabs: June 2026 Times/Press Herald/Siena Poll of the Likely Electorate in Maine
item.description: Results of a New York Times/Portland Press Herald/Siena poll of 608 likely voters conducted from June 19 to 26, 2026.

item.title: Toplines: June 2026 Times/Press Herald/Siena Poll of the Likely Electorate in Maine
item.description: Results of a New York Times/Portland Press Herald/Siena poll of 608 likely voters conducted from June 19 to 26, 2026.

item.title: Rescuers Find Earthquake Survivors as Critical Window Closes
item.description: An 11-year-old boy was rescued from the rubble of Venezuela’s twin earthquakes as the window to find survivors was closing. The country’s president said operations would continue.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times

item.title: Making New Friends Is Hard. These Social Clubs Want to Make It Easier.
item.description: A new type of event aims to forge connection despite hectic schedules, exhaustion and a plethora of other social options.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron and Emma Rose Milligan

media:content.media:description: Alana Johnson, an actor and comedian who was at the Critical Thinking Club gathering, suggested that New Yorkers should have more sex.

item.title: In Japan’s ‘Little Brazil,’ a World Cup Showdown Tests Loyalties
item.description: More than 200,000 Brazilians live in Japan, following more than a century of migration between the countries. Many are of Japanese descent, so who are they cheering on?
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

media:content.media:credit: Noriko Hayashi for The New York Times

item.title: Los jóvenes que eligieron quedarse en Cuba
item.description: En medio del mayor éxodo de la historia reciente del país, cuatro cubanos narran lo que significa permanecer en la isla.
item.dc:creator: Olivier Côté and Jean-François Bouchard

item.title: A.I. Is Strangling Our Economy
item.description: So much money is being poured into A.I. that the rest of the economy is starting to suffer.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer M. Harris

item.title: ‘Elle’ and 10 More Shows to Watch on TV this Week
item.description: This “Legally Blonde” prequel premieres and networks air coverage around the nation’s 250th Independence Day.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Lexi Minetree as Elle Woods in “Elle.”

item.title: Quote of the Day: Venezuelan Civilians Fill the Gaps One ‘Grain of Sand’ at a Time
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, June 29, 2026.
item.dc:creator: With

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 29, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Metropolitan Diary Challenge Day 2: How to Write Your N.Y. Story
item.description: We want to help you craft your own Metropolitan Diary entry. Here are some tips for turning a special New York memory into a great story.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan and Agnes Lee

item.title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3 Episode 2 Recap: Throne of Blood
item.description: Rhaenyra is plunged back into grief. What will that mean for the fate of the realm?
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Rhaenyra (Emma D’Arcy) suffered another major loss. Will it be in vain?

item.title: Oil Markets Edge Higher After Days of Attacks in Persian Gulf
item.description: Oil prices inched up on Sunday evening, while the S&P 500 futures market was little changed.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 29, 2026
item.description: Ginny Too and Rajeswari Rajamani make their New York Times Crossword collaboration debut.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Sam Hodgson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Are you set on today’s crossword? If not, we can help you with a bit of it.

item.title: Philippe Stern, Heir to the Patek Philippe Watch Brand, Dies at 88
item.description: In an era of electronic timepieces, he marketed $40,000 handmade watches as status symbols, breathing new life into a struggling industry.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

media:content.media:description: Philippe Stern in 1992. He was the third generation of his family to run the luxury watchmaker Patek Philippe and helped the Swiss watch industry survive the “quartz crisis.”

item.title: Mignon Dunn, Mezzo Soprano Known for ‘Carmen,’ Dies at 98
item.description: Growing up in Arkansas, she dreamed of singing at the Metropolitan Opera. She did so more than 650 times over 35 years.
item.dc:creator: Vivien Schweitzer

media:content.media:credit: Reg Innell/Toronto Star, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mignon Dunn in 1974 with Ermanno Mauro in a production of “Carmen” by the Canadian Opera Company in Toronto.

item.title: At a Caracas Morgue, Families and Officials Try to Identify More Than 100 Victims
item.description: Identifying victims has proved to be difficult because many bodies were badly crushed beneath collapsed buildings.
item.dc:creator: Isayen Herrera and Genevieve Glatsky

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A flyer of a missing family lays outside of the morgue of the Dr. José María Vargas Hospital on Friday.

item.title: Drone Footage Shows Devastation From Venezuela Earthquakes
item.description: Twin earthquakes that devastated Venezuela on Wednesday killed more than 1,400 people and left thousands more injured.
item.dc:creator: Zach Wasser

item.title: Europe’s Heat Politics
item.description: As the continent swelters, some right-wing populists are talking up their support for air-conditioning.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Kemi Badenoch, the leader of Britain’s Conservative Party, visited an oil rig in Aberdeen, Scotland, in March.

item.title: In Venezuela, a Community Comes Together to Search for Earthquake Survivors
item.description: Volunteers in a middle-class neighborhood in Caracas used drills, picks and hammers to break through concrete, trying to find anyone in need of rescue.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Ferrero and Julie Turkewitz

media:content.media:credit: Fabiola Ferrero for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rescue workers at the Residencia Rita in Caracas, Venezuela, on Saturday.

item.title: Delcy Rodríguez es acusada de politizar la ayuda tras los terremotos
item.description: Los críticos dicen que la presidenta interina del país, Delcy Rodríguez, está intentando sacar partido de la tragedia para su beneficio político. Sus seguidores acusan a la oposición de hacer lo mismo.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: Delcy Rodríguez en Caracas, en abril.

item.title: Three Firefighters Dead, as Wildfires Ravage Utah and Colorado
item.description: Three firefighters died and two were injured on Saturday in a blaze on the Utah-Colorado border, the U.S. Wildland Fire Service said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: A helicopter dropping water on the Cottonwood fire burning near Beaver, Utah, on Saturday.

item.title: Los Países Bajos registran su primer caso de muerte asistida de un niño de entre 1 y 12 años
item.description: La muerte asistida por un médico para niños con enfermedades terminales de ese grupo de edad está permitida en el país desde 2024.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses

media:content.media:description: Sophie Hermans, ministra de Salud, Bienestar y Deporte de los Países Bajos, en 2025.

item.title: Por qué lloran las personas mayores
item.description: Últimamente tengo tanto pasado a mis espaldas y dentro de mí que parece que se me sale por todas partes.
item.dc:creator: Roger Rosenblatt

item.title: Iran Risks Peace Talks With U.S. to Maintain Leverage Over Strait
item.description: Iran sees its control over the Strait of Hormuz as critical leverage in peace talks with the United States. It seems willing to risk the cease-fire to maintain that power.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Boats anchored off Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula near the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

item.title: La avalancha de voluntarios en Venezuela ralentiza las labores de rescate
item.description: El gran número de voluntarios que acudieron con ayuda después de los terremotos de Venezuela colapsó la única carretera que llevaba a la zona afectada, lo que retrasó la llegada de los equipos de rescate.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky, María Victoria Fermín and Fabiola Ferrero

media:content.media:credit: Fabiola Ferrero para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El equipo médico del Hospital Pérez de León en una camioneta en La Guaira, Venezuela, el viernes por la noche. Los médicos repartían comida, agua y suministros médicos a la gente en las calles.

item.title: Machado pide volver a Venezuela, lo que habría irritado a funcionarios de EE. UU.
item.description: María Corina Machado, la líder exiliada de la oposición venezolana, desea volver a casa. Funcionarios de EE. UU. consideran que su deseo es inoportuno.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Civiles buscan supervivientes en un edificio residencial colapsado en La Guaira, Venezuela, el jueves.

item.title: A ‘Bald Meet-Up’ in NYC Fosters a Sense of Empowerment
item.description: The gathering on Saturday in Washington Square Park brought words of affirmation and a community that many said they thought they’d never find.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A “Bald Meet-Up” in Washington Square Park on Saturday drew about 100 attendees who said they found solidarity in the gathering.

item.title: ‘Supergirl’ Falters at the Box Office, Testing DC Studios’ Reboot
item.description: Overall, ticket sales for the weekend were up 21 percent from last year. But the expensive “Supergirl” debuted as a disappointing second, with an estimated $38 million.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Deep Under the Rubble, Rescuers Find an 11-Year-Old Boy Alive
item.description: A Colombian rescue team worked for six hours to recover the child, Moises, from under nearly 10 feet of rubble in La Guaira. His rescue was captured on video.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times

item.title: L.G.B.T.Q. Figures Who Shaped History From the Margins
item.description: Their names may not be familiar, but they helped define queer art, activism and storytelling as we know it today.
item.dc:creator: Ash Wu and Amisha Padnani

item.title: A ‘Heat Dome’ Could Bring Triple- Digit Temperatures to the Midwest and Eastern U.S.
item.description: Forecasters warned that temperatures could break records in some areas. The heat is expected to last into the Fourth of July holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:description: Pedestrians on the Brooklyn Bridge during a heat wave in 2024. New York City, Philadelphia and Washington are likely to have days above 100 degrees this week, forecasters said.

item.title: Deadly Floods Hit Kentucky
item.description: Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky declared a state of emergency after deadly floods swept through parts of the state.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff and Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Greg Eans/The Messenger-Inquirer, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Drivers maneuvered their vehicles through flooded roads after a heavy rainfall in Owensboro, Ky., on Saturday.

item.title: Helicopter Crash Near Major Saudi Oil Refinery Kills 14 People
item.description: The incident occurred near the Ras Tanura facility in the east of the country, a key export hub for oil transported through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim

media:content.media:description: Parts of the Ras Tanura oil refinery were closed in March after a fire following an Iranian drone attack.

item.title: Five Bright and Summery Recipes for Lazy Vacation Days
item.description: Because vacation is even better when you get to cook.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Clark

media:content.media:credit: Linda Xiao for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.
media:content.media:description: Mark Bittman’s chicken kebabs with turmeric and chile.

item.title: Skydiving Plane Crashes in France, Killing All 11 People Aboard
item.description: Five instructors, five students and a pilot died when the plane crashed on Sunday morning in a field near the northeastern city of Nancy.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins and Leo Sands

media:content.media:description: Those aboard the plane that crashed had been scheduled to take part in tandem skydiving lessons, the mayor of Nancy told local news media.

item.title: Three Firefighters Die as Wildfires Ravage Utah and Colorado
item.description: Fires are raging in the arid Southwest after a warm winter. Forecasters say the fires may grow rapidly, and parts of Colorado have been ordered to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam, Jack Healy, Nancy Lofholm and Maia Spoto

media:content.media:description: The flag-draped body of one of the three wildland firefighters who died Saturday while battling a blaze was moved from a medevac helicopter to a waiting vehicle Sunday at Grand Junction Regional Airport.

item.title: Dozens of Dogs Join Search for Venezuela Earthquake Survivors
item.description: Specially trained dogs can pick up the scent of survivors beneath mounds of collapsed debris, indicating where clearance teams should begin digging.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands

media:content.media:description: A canine rescue unit from the Mexican army searching a damaged building in Caraballeda, Venezuela, on Friday.

item.title: These Are the Cold Noodles of the Summer
item.description: Melissa Clark’s nutty, gingery take is powerfully delicious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Weinstein

media:content.media:credit: Kelly Marshall for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Roscoe Betsill. Prop Stylist: Paige Hicks.

item.title: Addressing a Corrupt Pardon System
item.description: Readers respond to “There’s Actually Something We Can Do About Trump’s Pardons,” an Opinion guest essay by Tim Wu. Also: A loophole that harms our oceans.

item.title: Dear Diary
item.description: We celebrate 50 years of Metropolitan Diary.
item.dc:creator: Alex Vadukul

item.title: Heat Wave Starts to Break in Western Europe, as Central Europe Begins to Broil
item.description: After days of record temperatures in Western Europe, the extreme heat is easing there. Now, Central Europe is facing days of dangerous swelter.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

item.title: Rescuers in ‘Critical Hours’ as Window to Find Survivors Closes
item.description: More than three days after twin earthquakes devastated the Venezuelan coast, the rescue effort faced chaos and delays as the chances of finding survivors diminished.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
